channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India and Afghanistan to form Joint Working Group for Agriculture and Livestock Collaboration
item.description: India and Afghanistan have agreed to establish a Joint Working Group to develop a long-term roadmap for cooperation in agriculture and livestock. The decision was made during bilateral talks between U...

item.title: Meta invests $13B in Alberta's first Canadian data centre, creating thousands of jobs
item.description: Meta, the technology giant behind Facebook and Instagram, has announced plans to build its first Canadian data centre northeast of Edmonton, in Sturgeon County. The project, valued at over $13 billion...

item.title: Telstra Outage Disrupts Train Services Across Australia
item.description: A major Telstra network outage earlier this week disrupted not only telecommunications but also critical infrastructure, including train services in Victoria and New South Wales. The incident highligh...

item.title: Five SAPS Officers Face Corruption Charges After Alleged Bribery Demand
item.description: Five members of the South African Police Service (SAPS) attached to the Vanderbijlpark Visible Policing Task Team have appeared in court on corruption charges after allegedly demanding a R100,000 brib...

item.title: Improved Rice Seeds Boost Economic Growth in Mbarali District
item.description: Farmers in Tanzania’s Mbarali district are experiencing significant economic growth due to the adoption of improved rice seed varieties. These high-quality seeds, resistant to pests and diseases, have...

item.title: Judge Orders Trump to Pay E. Jean Carroll After Legal Battle
item.description: A federal judge ruled against Donald Trump's emergency motion to halt payments to accuser E. Jean Carroll, who successfully sued him for defamation and sexual assault. The court ordered Trump to pay $...

item.title: US-Iran Escalation: Strikes on Iran Trigger Attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain
item.description: The United States launched fresh military strikes on Iran to secure the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global oil route, which led to retaliatory attacks by Iran on Kuwait and Bahrain. The conflict, which ...

item.title: AFEA 2026 Conference Explores African Development Strategies
item.description: The African Finance and Economics Association (AFEA) held its 2026 conference, bringing together academics, policymakers, and industry experts to discuss critical issues shaping Africa's economic and ...

item.title: DOJ warns state officials of criminal charges over voter fraud concerns
item.description: The US Department of Justice (DOJ) has issued letters to election officials in all 50 states, warning of potential criminal charges for knowingly allowing noncitizens to vote or remain on voter rolls....

item.title: Faithful Literary Adaptations: A List of 8 Accurate Film Retellings
item.description: Adapting classic literature into film is a complex task, requiring a delicate balance between fidelity to the source material and cinematic storytelling. The article explores eight adaptations praised...

item.title: Egypt Complain Over Referee Errors That Led To World Cup Exit
item.description: The Egyptian Football Federation, led by President Hany Aburida, has officially filed a complaint with FIFA against French referee Francois Letexier and his team for alleged serious refereeing mistake...

item.title: SIT investigates alleged temple donation misappropriation in Ayodhya
item.description: The investigation into alleged misappropriation of donations at the Ram Janmabhoomi Temple has entered a crucial phase as Ayodhya Police questioned three key accused—Lavkush Mishra, Anukalp Mishra, an...

item.title: Post-FIFA World Cup: Vancouver's Transformations and Ongoing Adjustments
item.description: Vancouver is undergoing several changes following the conclusion of the FIFA World Cup. Temporary event signage, including banners and wayfinding markers, will be removed from the Last Mile spectator ...

item.title: Nigeria's AGF Fagbemi Faces Allegations Of Using State Power To Target Rep Member Amobi Ogah In Political Dispute
item.description: The article details allegations against Nigeria's Attorney-General of the Federation (AGF), Lateef Fagbemi, of abusing prosecutorial powers to target Rep. Amobi Ogah, a member of the House of Represen...

item.title: Website access error: Reader blocked by bot detection system
item.description: The article appears to be an error message from News Corp Australia's website, indicating that the user has been blocked as a crawler bot. The message explains that the site uses software to manage bo...

item.title: Maine Senate Candidate Drops Out Amid Sexual Assault Allegations
item.description: Democratic Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner ended his campaign after facing allegations of sexual assault, marking a significant setback for his bid. The controversy emerged following a POLITICO ...

item.title: Blue Alert sa Metro Manila dahil sa Pagdating ng Typhoon Inday
item.description: Ang Metro Manila ay nasa Blue Alert status dahil sa posibleng epekto ng Typhoon Inday, ayon sa Office of the Civil Defense (OCD). Ang OCD ay nagsimula na sa virtual emergency operation center para mag...

item.title: Blue Jays Dominate Giants with 10-0 Win, Cease Nears No-Hitter
item.description: Toronto Blue Jays secured a commanding 10-0 victory over the San Francisco Giants, with Dylan Cease delivering eight no-hit innings before allowing a single to Heliot Ramos in the ninth. Cease (6-4) s...

item.title: Mervyn Naidoo Recognized for Excellence in Hard News Reporting at 2026 Sikuvile Awards
item.description: Mervyn Naidoo, the Daily News' Content Manager, has been honoured with the 2026 Standard Bank Sikuvile Journalism Award in the 'Hard News' category. The prestigious event, co-hosted by SANEF and Stand...

item.title: Legal Practice Council Seeks to Strike Busisiwe Mkhwebane Off Advocates’ Roll Over Alleged Misconduct
item.description: The South African Legal Practice Council has formally requested the Pretoria High Court to remove former public protector and politician Busisiwe Mkhwebane from the advocates' roll, citing severe char...
